notes: connectorType: "DATABRICKS". Dialect fields embed under `config.databricks` (sibling of `config.snowflake`, `config.postgres`).

authStrategy=`service_role` covers the shared-credential Databricks modes (Access Token and Client Credentials). The server discriminates Databricks modes by which nested `databricksAuth.*` variant is populated, not by `authStrategy` (matches the Snowflake pattern where Password + Key Pair both use `service_role`).

PAT credential lives at `config.databricks.databricksAuth.pat.token` — two-level nesting. `databricksAuth` is a oneof-style discriminator with three valid siblings: `pat` (Access Token), `clientCredentials` (Client Credentials), `oauthU2m` (both OAuth SSO and OAuth Individual).
UI-required fields: name, host, httpPath, port, catalog, schema, token. No optional fields in Access Token mode — all seven are required. Port defaults to 443 and is sent as integer.

`host` is the Databricks workspace hostname without scheme. `httpPath` is the SQL warehouse path (format `/sql/1.0/warehouses/<id>`). Both are copy-paste fields from the Databricks SQL warehouse 'Connection details' panel.
Pre-create behavior: UI fires TestConnector first with the identical body; on 200 it fires CreateConnector. Both accept the same body shape.

GetConnector persisted shape: `connector.databricksMetadata.{host, httpPath, port, databricksAuth, catalog, schema}`. `databricksAuth.pat` is returned as an empty object `{}` — the token is never echoed. `connector.authStrategy` is echoed at top level.
Wire name asymmetry: `databricks` on the request side and `databricksMetadata` on the response side (same pattern Snowflake uses: `snowflake` → `snowflakeMetadata`).
Databricks auth-mode UI tabs (in order): Access Token, Client Credentials, OAuth (SSO), OAuth (Individual). Access Token is the default.
